reasoning: While programmatic signals show excellent content preservation (shingling recall 0.992), the LLM fidelity check identifies two major visual defects. The presence of solid black horizontal bars as rendering artifacts and the total loss of primary organizational branding (NC DIT logo) creates a significant visual-structural deviation from the source. Given the 3x cost of false negatives, human review is required to evaluate if these visual artifacts impede accessibility or professional standards.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains large, solid black horizontal bars throughout the document (e.g., after the main header, between item 5 and item 6, and after item 9) which are not present in the source PDF. These bars appear to be misrendered artifacts from page headers or dividers and significantly disrupt the visual organization and flow compared to the original document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF shows a clean layout with page breaks and logos, but no solid black horizontal bars.
concerns.output_quote: Thick black horizontal bars appear multiple times, notably before item 1, after the table for item 5, and after item 9.
concerns.location: Throughout the HTML document at section transitions corresponding to PDF page breaks.

concerns.analysis: The primary organizational logo ('NC DIT 911 Board'), which is present at the top of every page in the source PDF, is entirely missing from the HTML output. While some text identifying the board is present, the specific branding and graphical elements of the logo are absent. The red 'Approved' watermark is also missing, though this is considered an acceptable decorative difference.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo is present in the header of pages 1, 2, 3, and 4 of the PDF.
concerns.output_quote: The logo image is missing from the header of the HTML document.
concerns.location: Top of the document and at the start of major sections corresponding to PDF page breaks.

action_items: Verify if the black horizontal bars interfere with screen reader navigation or general readability.
Restore the missing organizational logo to ensure proper government branding.
Investigate the cause of the rendering artifacts to prevent recurrence in similar layouts.
